deep_dive.title: GuidedBridge: Training-freely Improving Bridge Models with Prior Guidance
deep_dive.abstract: Guidance methods, such as classifier-free guidance (CFG) and auto-guidance (AG), have advanced noise-to-data generation in diffusion models. Recently, bridge models have introduced a data-to-data generative process that can exploit an instructive clean prior. In this work, inspired by previous methods creating quality difference between denoising results as guidance, we propose a training-free bridge guidance method, termed Prior Guidance (PG). Specifically, we introduce a weak prior, which is unseen during bridge pre-training, hindering prior exploitation and thereby degrading denoising result. Then, we contrast it with the seen prior to highlight and enhance prior exploitation via a scaling factor. Moreover, we analyze the underlying mechanism of prior exploitation in the bridge process and design frequency-modulated prior guidance (FMPG), which tailors the guidance scale to low- and high-frequency bands coherent with bridge generative dynamics. To address prior exploitation in image in-painting, we develop a cascaded framework, CFG-FMPG, which first generates a noisy hidden representation via CFG and then exploits it as a generative prior with FMPG, fulfilling their complementary strengths without compromising inference efficiency. Experiments demonstrate that our PG methods consistently improve pre-trained bridge models across diverse image translation tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes training-free guidance method for bridge models (data-to-data generative process)
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出针对桥模型的无训练引导方法（数据到数据生成过程）
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出针对桥模型的无训练引导方法（数据到数据生成过程）
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出针对桥模型的无训练引导方法（数据到数据生成过程）
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GuidedBridge introduces Prior Guidance, a training-free method to improve bridge models by contrasting weak and seen priors.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 桥模型能利用干净先验进行数据到数据生成，但现有引导方法需要训练。本文提出无训练的先前引导（PG），通过引入一个未见过的弱先验来产生质量差异，然后与见过的先验对比并缩放，从而增强先验利用。方法无需额外训练，实验表明在多个数据集上提升了桥模型生成质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 桥模型能利用干净先验进行数据到数据生成，但现有引导方法需要训练。本文提出无训练的先前引导（PG），通过引入一个未见过的弱先验来产生质量差异，然后与见过的先验对比并缩放，从而增强先验利用。方法无需额外训练，实验表明在多个数据集上提升了桥模型生成质量。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: diffusion bridge connecting two probability distributions

deep_dive.title: Nonlocal Mean Field Schrödinger Bridge with Learned Interactions
deep_dive.abstract: The Schrödinger Bridge Problem constructs a stochastic process that connects an initial distribution to a terminal distribution with minimum energy. This work considers its mean-field extension, the Mean-Field Schrödinger Bridge, for interacting particle systems. With nonlocal interactions, evaluating the resulting particle-dependent distributional terms can scale quadratically with the population size, which makes large-scale problems intractable. We address this bottleneck by approximating the nonlocal interactions with neural network surrogates. The resulting four-stage alternating algorithm reduces the per-step cost from quadratic to linear in the population size at inference. We also derive Grönwall-type stability bounds that show how surrogate errors propagate to the generated trajectories. In numerical experiments on navigation and opinion-dynamics tasks, the proposed method reproduces trajectories obtained with analytical evaluation and reduces training time.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Solves the nonlocal Mean Field Schrodinger Bridge with learned interactions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用学习交互求解非局部均场薛定谔桥
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用学习交互求解非局部均场薛定谔桥
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用学习交互求解非局部均场薛定谔桥
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Nonlocal Mean Field Schrodinger Bridge uses neural network surrogates to reduce per-step cost from quadratic to linear for interacting particle systems.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 均场薛定谔桥问题中非局部交互导致计算量随粒子数平方增长。本文提出用神经网络替代非局部交互项，设计四阶段交替算法，将推理步成本从二次降为线性。还导出了Grönwall型稳定性界，证明代理误差可控。实验在大规模粒子系统中验证了效率提升。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 均场薛定谔桥问题中非局部交互导致计算量随粒子数平方增长。本文提出用神经网络替代非局部交互项，设计四阶段交替算法，将推理步成本从二次降为线性。还导出了Grönwall型稳定性界，证明代理误差可控。实验在大规模粒子系统中验证了效率提升。

deep_dive.title: Optimal Transport Flow Matching by Design
deep_dive.abstract: Flow matching models learn to transport samples from a simple prior distribution to a complex data distribution. When prior-data pairs are coupled via optimal transport (OT), the learned trajectories are straight and non-crossing, enabling fast, even single-step, generation. However, computing the OT coupling in high dimensions is intractable, and existing methods attempt to solve the OT problem, at the cost of persistent bias or significant overhead. Rather than solving for the OT coupling, we reformulate the problem. Once the prior is treated as a design choice rather than a fixed input, the OT coupling between prior and data is no longer unique. Many priors admit an OT-optimal identity coupling to the data, leaving us free to choose one that is also tractable to sample. We identify low-frequency projection of natural images as such a choice. The identity coupling between data and its low-frequency representation is empirically OT-optimal, the prior is structured enough to be sampled by a lightweight model at inference, and the remaining flow-matching task reduces to synthesizing high-frequency detail. Interpolating the prior with Gaussian noise further improves generation quality while preserving the OT coupling. The approach requires no modifications to the flow model itself, and integrates naturally with latent-space models, classifier-free guidance, and one-step generation frameworks. Across all benchmarks, our method reduces trajectory curvature by more than $2\times$ compared to existing flow matching methods, yielding better generation quality in the few-step regime.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Optimal transport coupling in flow matching for generative modeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 流匹配中的最优传输耦合
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 流匹配中的最优传输耦合
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 流匹配中的最优传输耦合
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper shows that by designing the prior distribution, the optimal transport coupling between prior and data can be made identity, enabling efficient flow matching with straight trajectories.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对流匹配模型中最优传输耦合计算昂贵的问题，本文提出通过设计先验分布而非求解最优传输问题，使得先验与数据之间的最优传输耦合成为恒等映射。该方法避免了高维最优传输的计算开销，同时保持了轨迹的直线性和非交叉性。实验表明，该设计能够在单步生成中达到与复杂最优传输方法相当的性能。该工作为生成模型中的传输路径优化提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对流匹配模型中最优传输耦合计算昂贵的问题，本文提出通过设计先验分布而非求解最优传输问题，使得先验与数据之间的最优传输耦合成为恒等映射。该方法避免了高维最优传输的计算开销，同时保持了轨迹的直线性和非交叉性。实验表明，该设计能够在单步生成中达到与复杂最优传输方法相当的性能。该工作为生成模型中的传输路径优化提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: A New Perspective on Reverse Diffusion for Monte Carlo Sampling
quick_skim.abstract: This paper introduces a novel perspective on the use of reverse diffusion processes for sampling from unnormalized densities. The central idea is to embed the target density as the marginal at the initial time of a suitably constructed diffusion process evolving over a finite horizon. In contrast to existing approaches, the proposed methodology involves neither time discretization error nor score function estimation, so that Monte Carlo variability is the only source of approximation. A key theoretical result characterizes the Radon-Nikodym derivative of the reverse diffusion transition distribution with respect to that of an Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) process. This representation provides a tractable change-of-measure formulation and serves as the foundation for two distinct classes of Monte Carlo algorithms. The first class approximates the reverse transition distribution via a sequence of pseudo-marginal Metropolis-Hastings MCMC algorithms. The resulting scheme produces an approximate i.i.d. sample from the target distribution and is fully parallelizable, as trajectories can be generated independently. The second class consists of MCMC algorithms targeting the joint law of the whole diffusion path in $[0,T]$, for a suitably chosen horizon $T$. The proposed samplers combine three types of updates. One update simulates the diffusion forward in time according to an OU dynamics, conditional on its initial value. The remaining two update the backward component via Metropolis-type steps: one conditions on the terminal value at time $T$ and the other one does not. In both cases, acceptance probabilities are implemented using Barker-type Bernoulli factory constructions. The proposed methods perform well for targets with multimodality and complex dependence structures, providing a scalable and efficient alternative to the widely used random-walk Metropolis algorithm.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: constructs a reverse diffusion process from target density to OU process, effectively building a diffusion bridge
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 构建从目标密度到OU过程的逆向扩散，实质是建立了扩散桥
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 构建从目标密度到OU过程的逆向扩散，实质是建立了扩散桥
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 构建从目标密度到OU过程的逆向扩散，实质是建立了扩散桥
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a reverse diffusion method for sampling from unnormalized densities by embedding the target as initial marginal, avoiding time discretization and score estimation, effectively constructing a diffusion bridge.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种新的逆向扩散采样方法，将目标密度嵌入为一个有限时间扩散过程的初始边际分布，从而构建了一个从目标分布到Ornstein-Uhlenbeck过程的扩散桥。与传统方法不同，该方法避免了时间离散化误差和得分函数估计，仅引入蒙特卡洛变异性。关键理论结果给出了逆向扩散转移分布相对于OU过程的Radon-Nikodym导数，为可处理的测度变换提供了基础。该方法为构建任意两个概率分布之间的扩散桥提供了新思路，在生成建模和蒙特卡洛采样中有重要潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了一种新的逆向扩散采样方法，将目标密度嵌入为一个有限时间扩散过程的初始边际分布，从而构建了一个从目标分布到Ornstein-Uhlenbeck过程的扩散桥。与传统方法不同，该方法避免了时间离散化误差和得分函数估计，仅引入蒙特卡洛变异性。关键理论结果给出了逆向扩散转移分布相对于OU过程的Radon-Nikodym导数，为可处理的测度变换提供了基础。该方法为构建任意两个概率分布之间的扩散桥提供了新思路，在生成建模和蒙特卡洛采样中有重要潜力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Constructing a diffusion bridge between two arbitrary probability distributions
